summary:
Only the declared output directory is new — no other files were touched.

## Summary

I read the full upstream context (business requirements R1–R50, Define-phase discovery notes, the workflow plan, and the confirmed/open questions), then grounded every design decision in the actual codebase: existing models/enums, `StatusHelper`, the `pipelines`/`workers` index views, the `BroadcastCard`/`BroadcastColumn` pattern, `Phases::AnswerQuestions`, and both guides. I wrote `output/design.md`, which specifies: three new `Dashboard::` query objects (active pipelines, recent activity, fleet health) with concrete derivations for gaps the codebase has today (e.g. `pipeline.status` never actually reaches `"blocked"`/`"stuck"`, so "needs attention" is derived from step_run state instead); a `Dashboard::Broadcast` service plus a per-user Turbo stream to make the aggregate dashboard live, wired through exactly 8 precise call sites (2 centralized via the existing broadcast primitives, 6 targeted at event-creation points); a new structured `open_questions_structured` artifact (with a `db/seeds.rb` and `docs/artifact-schema.md` change) to make the R36–R50 answer-questions modal render one input per question reliably, built on native `<dialog>` for accessibility; and a full file-level plan (new files, modified files, test plan, and a requirements-traceability table). It also proposes two guide additions per CLAUDE.md's rule for when a guide is silent.
